frames.name: Diplomatic Coordination Frame
frames.description: Presents the meeting as diplomatic coordination between allies during wartime, emphasizing unity and cooperation
frames.emphasis: US-Germany alliance, shared goals on Iran, and bilateral cooperation

frames.name: European Division Frame
frames.description: Highlights divisions within Europe regarding support for US actions against Iran
frames.emphasis: Mixed European support, with some countries helpful (Germany) and others uncooperative (Spain, UK)

frames.name: Military Progress Frame
frames.description: Focuses on Trump's claims about military success against Iran's capabilities
frames.emphasis: Iran's military infrastructure being 'knocked out' and US military effectiveness

frames.name: Multi-Crisis Management Frame
frames.description: Presents the meeting as addressing multiple global conflicts simultaneously
frames.emphasis: Iran, Ukraine, and trade issues as interconnected challenges requiring coordination

outlierPerspectives: DW's focus on Merz pushing back on Trump's Ukraine ceasefire proposals that would cede Ukrainian territory
Trump's claim that he 'might have forced Israel's hand' rather than being pulled into war by Israel
NYT's framing of the meeting as originally planned but now overshadowed by Iran conflict
missingContext: Specific details about what military actions have actually occurred against Iran
Timeline of how the Iran conflict began and escalated
Concrete details about European countries' specific positions and contributions
Background on previous Trump-Merz meetings and their outcomes
Details about the trade agreements being discussed
Verification of Trump's claims about Iran's military capabilities being destroyed
